name: Rpc
comment:
This is a runtime to support rpcplus generated clients. Usage:

    import MyProtocol (..)
    import Rpc
    import Signal
    import WebSocket

    -- Request Signal
    rpcRequest : Signal.Channel Request
    rpcRequest = Signal.channel NoRequest

    -- Response Signal
    rpcResponse : Signal.Signal Response
    rpcResponse =
        Rpc.connect protocol
            (WebSocket.connect "wss://myserver.com:443/rpcplus")
            (Signal.subscribe rpcRequest)

    -- Send a request
    Signal.send rpcRequest NoRequest

# Usage
@docs connect, Transport

aliases.name: ConnectionMixin
aliases.type: { protocol | serverChannel : Channel String, responseChannel : Channel response, action : Signal (Action request), state : State request }

aliases.name: Protocol
aliases.type: { method : request -> String, params : request -> JsonValue, noRequest : request, noResponse : response, closeStream : request -> Maybe Id, codec : ResponseEnvelope -> request -> response, done : response -> Bool }

aliases.name: RequestEnvelope
aliases.type: { id : Id, method : String, params : JsonValue }

aliases.name: Response
aliases.type: { id : Id, input : input, output : Error output }

aliases.name: ResponseEnvelope
aliases.type: { id : Id, result : Maybe JsonValue, error : Maybe String }

aliases.name: State
aliases.type: { pendingRequest : Dict Id request, nextId : Id }

aliases.name: Transport
aliases.comment:
Transport is generally provided by WebSocket.connect.  In the future this
same interface may support retry logic or other transports (for example,
Http-based transports).

values.name: connect
values.comment:
connect overlays a Protocol, which is type-safe, over a Transport, which
carries strings and is usually provided by WebSocket.connect.  The
resulting connection will read from the request signal and write to the
response signal.
values.type: Protocol request response -> Transport -> Signal request -> Signal response

values.name: requestEnvelope
values.type: Connection request response -> request -> Id -> RequestEnvelope

values.name: step
values.type: Connection request response -> Action request -> State request -> State request

values.name: stepClientInput
values.type: Connection request response -> request -> State request -> State request

values.name: stepServerOutput
values.type: Connection request response -> String -> State request -> State request
